questions.question: Find the GCF of $12$ and $18$ using factors.
Find the LCM of $9$ and $12$ using multiples.
A basketball player scores $15$ points in one game and $20$ points in another. Find the GCF of these scores.
A cyclist rides $8$ miles in one hour and $12$ miles in another hour. Find the LCM of these distances.
Find the prime factorization of $48$ to determine its GCF with $18$.
A football team plays $10$ games in one season and $15$ games in another. Find the LCM of these game counts.
A tennis player wins $9$ sets in one tournament and $12$ sets in another. Find the GCF of these set counts.
Find the LCM of $8$ and $15$ using prime factorization.
A swimmer competes in $12$ events in one meet and $18$ events in another. Find the GCF and LCM of these event counts and explain their significance in this context.

A golfer plays $9$ holes in one round and $12$ holes in another. Find the prime factorization of these hole counts and use it to determine their GCF and LCM. Provide a real-world application of this problem.

teacher_rules: Emphasize the importance of understanding the concepts of GCF and LCM.
Use real-world examples to illustrate the applications of GCF and LCM.
Encourage students to use prime factorization to find GCF and LCM.
